channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As tariffs put trade between China and the US in peril, Chinese businesses ponder the future
item.description: The 145% tariff from the U.S. and the retaliatory 125% tariff from China are putting businesses doing trade between the U.S. and China on edge.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: America's news TV channel for the Mideast fires its staff after funding cuts
item.description: The head of the U.S.-funded Al Hurra Arabic-language television and online news outlet has terminated most of his staff and curtailed TV programming.
item.dc:creator: Sam Magdy And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court gives Trump wiggle room with mistaken deportation decision
item.description: A Supreme Court decision pushing for the return of a man mistakenly deported to a Salvadoran prison has both his lawyers and the Trump administration claiming victory, leaving unclear if and when Kilmar Abrego Garcia will return home.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Whitmer ditches Dem playbook on Trump's tariffs amid 2028 speculation
item.description: Democratic Gov. Gretchen Whitmer of MIchigan bucked the party line during a trip to the White House Wednesday amid speculation about her 2028 campaign aspirations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Tim Scott outlines first 100 days of Congress, crypto, tariffs
item.description: Senate Banking Committee chairman Tim Scott described the first hundred days of Congress, a trade war with China and the timeline for implementing critical crypto policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Take a trip to Ohio to learn about William McKinley, Trump's much-admired Gilded Age president
item.description: If there’s one place where interest in William McKinley has remained unwavering, it's in the 25th president's native northeast Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?” Luna, then a first-time mom and first-term lawmaker, could no longer fly to Washington to cast votes in
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump proclaims himself 'in good shape,' but the results of his physical aren't immediately released
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump had an annual physical Friday and concluded, “I did well,” praising his own heart, soul and cognitive ability while noting medical reports from White House doctors may not be ready until the weekend. The 78-year-old, who in January became the oldest in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS ends protections from deportation for Afghanistan, Cameroon
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has stripped protections from deportation for Afghans and Cameroonians in the U.S., calling into question the ability of some Afghan evacuees to remain in America.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Laredo OKs giving state city land to build border wall
item.description: The City of Laredo is allowing the State of Texas a small section of city-owned land near an international bridge to build a segment of border wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration scores win as judge clears way for ICE enforcement in churches, places of worship
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that ICE agents can make arrests at churches and other places of worship, rejecting claims from religious groups of rights violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge sides with Trump in allowing immigration enforcement in houses of worship
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday sided with the Trump administration in allowing immigration agents to conduct enforcement operations at houses of worship for now, despite a lawsuit filed by religious groups over the new policy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge hammers DOJ on whereabouts of alleged MS-13 gang member following SCOTUS order
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland sharply questioned DOJ attorneys over the location of an alleged MS-13 gang member who was deported to El Salvador last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: White House responds to Supreme Court ruling on mistaken deportation
item.description: The White House on Friday responded to the Supreme Court’s ruling that the Trump administration must “facilitate” the return of a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rather than "effectuate" the return.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SCOOP: Biden-era grant program described as 'gold bar' scheme by Trump EPA administrator under scrutiny
item.description: House lawmakers are seeking answers on whether a controversial Biden-era green energy grant program was used improperly to boost groups with ties to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Head of US Space Force base in Greenland is fired after Vance visit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The commander of a U.S. Space Force base in Greenland has been fired after she sent a base-wide email breaking with official messaging following Vice President JD Vance’s visit to the Danish territory that President Donald Trump is seeking to annex.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bessent moves to center of Trump World
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has stepped up his role on President Trump’s economic team, taking center stage on the president’s plans for tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic prosecutor in crime-torn Albuquerque launches bid for New Mexico governor
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A Democratic district attorney in the eye of New Mexico’s tempest over crime and accountability launched his candidacy for governor Thursday as the National Guard prepares to deploy to the state’s largest city to shore up public safety.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: “In his short time in government, Elon Musk has done enormous harm to working Americans,” House Democrats said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: As cost of new products rises, many thrifty shoppers look to secondhand
item.description: President Trump has said tariffs will counter imbalance in the country’s trade partnerships, but experts have warned this could lead to higher costs for American businesses and consumers — which could mean more people shop secondhand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders White House to allow AP access to news events
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor N. McFadden rules the White House cannot deny the Associated Press access to news events because the wire service continues to use “Gulf of Mexico” rather than “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
